name: Drex (Brazilian Wholesale Tokenization Platform)
chain: Originally Hyperledger Besu (pivoted to centralized architecture in 2025)

chain_infra.name: Originally permissioned Hyperledger Besu; pivoting to centralized layer with tokenization on top (2025)

summary: Brazil's central bank tokenization platform, originally framed as 'Digital Real CBDC' with 16+ industry participants (Visa, Santander, Mastercard, Microsoft, etc.) testing tokenized deposit + government bond use cases. Major pivot announced 2025: BCB acknowledged blockchain privacy could not meet LGPD requirements at acceptable throughput (under 10 TPS with privacy vs 300+ TPS without). Phase 3 (announced June 2025) refocuses on tokenized credit, guarantees, and lien reconciliation — explicitly de-emphasizing retail CBDC and public blockchain. Important case study in central bank tokenization grappling with privacy-vs-programmability tradeoffs.

sources.title: Ledger Insights: Drex pilot phase 1 report (February 2025)
Human Rights Foundation CBDC Tracker: Brazil (March 2026 update)

underlying_asset: Phase 1-2: Brazilian government bonds (LTN), tokenized commercial bank deposits, wholesale CBDC. Phase 3 onward: credit collateral and lien reconciliation focus.
reserve_composition: Per use case; wholesale CBDC is direct BCB liability.

timeline.event: Drex Pilot Phase 1 launched (originally branded as Digital Real CBDC)
Phase 2 launched with 16+ participants and expanded use cases
BCB publishes Phase 1 report flagging privacy and scaling challenges
BCB pivots: Phase 3 refocuses on tokenized credit collateral, drops public-facing retail CBDC
BCB acknowledges Drex 'is not exactly a CBDC' in traditional sense
BCB official Fraga: 'we don't need a retail CBDC' — Drex repositioned as wholesale tokenization platform
